core_payload.content:
# Symphony Project

## 1. Role
Symphony Project is the concrete N1Hub engineering stream that delivers repository orchestration in the live codebase. It routes that delivery work through four bounded sub-hubs so assignment intake, live session fabric, workflow safety, and run observability remain explicit instead of collapsing into one 16-link project hub.

## 2. Routing Surfaces
- `assignment-intake` owns tracker-fed intake, workspace entry, swarm-fed assignments, and capsule-job routing before live execution begins.
- `live-session-fabric` owns service runtime, workspace lifecycle, harness protocol, and assignment continuity while a run is active.
- `workflow-safety` owns workflow law, approval and sandbox posture, night-shift gating, and the testing or security guardrails around execution.
- `run-observability` owns attempts, retries, status projection, lineage, and the evidence surfaces that explain what happened and what must happen next.

## 3. Parent Boundary
The parent Symphony Project hub keeps only the top-level relationship to CapsuleOS platform work, the Symphony foundation contract, and the four project delivery sub-hubs. Detailed implementation routing now flows through those sub-hubs so the project remains a legible delivery map instead of a flat registry of every runtime atom, control surface, and integration lane.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Symphony Project routes repository delivery through four bounded surfaces: assignment intake, live session fabric, workflow safety, and run observability. The parent keeps only top-level relationships to the CapsuleOS platform, the Symphony foundation contract, and the four project sub-hubs. This makes project doctrine more legible because intake, runtime continuity, workflow law, and recovery evidence no longer hide inside one overloaded project capsule. The result is a clearer implementation contract for how repository orchestration is delivered in code.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Removed meta-language so Symphony Project states present delivery doctrine directly.
Refactored Symphony Project into four bounded sub-hubs so assignment intake, live session fabric, workflow safety, and run observability no longer collapse into a 16-link project hub.
